commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated code repair remains; the next action is maintainer compatibility sign-off for the fail-closed cap plus normal merge gating.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the patch reuses in-repo bounded-read/error helpers, adds no dependencies or scripts, and improves raw URL redaction. (sha=f9915d6ffe13adc79087b28e8af9cbca9a547304)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds `openclaw capability video generate` no-output provider URL downloads with `readResponseWithLimit`, redacts failed download URLs, and adds CLI regression tests for overflow, under-cap, configured-cap, empty-body, and HTTP-failure cases.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +228. Total +251 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and v2026.6.10 both fetch provider video URLs and buffer the no-output path with `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())`, and the PR tests drive that same CLI path with streamed oversized bodies.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair remains; the next action is maintainer compatibility sign-off for the fail-closed cap plus normal merge gating.

target.title: fix(cli): bound in-memory video download from provider URLs
target.body:
<!-- Related: the #95103 / #95108 / #95218 / #96495 / #96873 response-limit campaign -->

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where running `openclaw capability video generate` **without** `--output` could exhaust process memory when a provider returns a video URL instead of inline bytes. In that fallback path the CLI downloaded the provider-supplied (untrusted, external) URL with a single unbounded `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())`. Generated videos routinely run to tens of MiB, so a large — or hostile/buggy — response would be buffered whole into memory, risking an OOM crash of the CLI/agent process.

The `--output` branch is unaffected: it already streams straight to disk and is the safe path.

## Why This Change Was Made

The buffered fallback now reads under a byte cap using the project's existing shared helper `readResponseWithLimit` (`@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit`), the same bounded-read primitive already used for generated video/audio/image downloads (e.g. `src/video-generation/dashscope-compatible.ts`, `src/music-generation/provider-assets.ts`). The cap comes from `resolveGeneratedMediaMaxBytes(cfg, "video")` — the per-kind default `MAX_VIDEO_BYTES` (16 MiB), overridable by the user via `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb`. The read is **fail-closed**: on overflow the helper cancels the body stream and throws, rather than silently truncating a partial/corrupt video.

The overflow error reports **only the provider label and the byte cap** — it never echoes the raw `video.url`, which may be a signed/tokenized download URL carrying access material. This matches the sibling generated-media downloaders (`vydra generated video download exceeds <N> bytes; pass --output to stream large videos to disk`). No public API, flag, config schema, or default behavior changes for normal-sized downloads.

## User Impact

- **CLI users**: an oversized or malicious provider video URL can no longer OOM the process on the no-`--output` path; they get a clear, actionable error (which points to `--output`) instead. Videos at or under the cap (default 16 MiB) behave exactly as before. For larger legitimate videos, `--output` streams to disk with no in-memory limit.
- **Operators running agents**: removes an unbounded-allocation vector driven by external response size; the cap is tunable via `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb`.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof (not mock-only)

Driven against a **real `node:http` server** bound to `127.0.0.1` that streams the body in 1 MiB chunks with **no `Content-Length`**, exercising the **real exported** `readResponseWithLimit` helper (the exact primitive this fix calls) on Node v22.22.0. Includes a **negative control** (the OLD unbounded path) measuring bytes-on-wire:

```
=== REAL-BEHAVIOR PROOF (PR #97549) — Node v22.22.0 ===
  ok: default video cap is 16 MiB (got 16777216)
  ok: mediaMaxMb=2 override resolves to 2 MiB (got 2097152)
  ok: bounded read rejected oversized body
  ok: overflow error is provider+cap, URL-free (got: vydra generated video download exceeds 16777216 bytes)
  ok: bounded read pulled near the 16 MiB cap, not the full 64 MiB (wire=19922944)
  ok: negative control: OLD unbounded read pulled the FULL ~64 MiB (buffered=67108864, wire=67108864)
  ok: cap is load-bearing: unbounded read >> bounded read (67108864 vs 19922944)
  ok: small under-cap body returned intact (got: small-video-bytes)

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

The bounded read cancels the stream after ~19.9 MiB (just past the 16 MiB cap) while the OLD unbounded `arrayBuffer()` path drains the **full 67,108,864 bytes** — a **3.4x gap** proving the cap is load-bearing.

### Focused tests

Added to `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` using **only that file's existing local mocks** — the shared `provider-http-test-mocks.ts` was intentionally **NOT** touched. Four tests drive the real `runVideoGenerate` via `registerCapabilityCli`:

1. **fails closed when an url-only generated video exceeds the in-memory byte cap** — a test-local `ReadableStream` would emit 64 MiB if drained; the command exits 1 with `vydra generated video download exceeds 16777216 bytes`, the stream's `cancel()` hook fires (`canceled === true`), and `enqueued <= 18` of 64 proves the body was never fully buffered. **Security regression guard**: asserts the error does **NOT** contain the URL or its `?sig=secret-presigned-token` query.
2. **buffers an url-only generated video that stays under the byte cap** — a small response on the same no-`--output` path returns normally (`outputs.length === 1`), no overflow error.
3. **honors a smaller configured mediaMaxMb cap** — with `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb = 2`, the cap resolves to 2 MiB (`2097152`) and the reader cancels even earlier (`enqueued <= 4`), proving the cap is config-driven.
4. **buffers an empty-body url-only generated video without error** — 0-byte boundary returns cleanly.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  103 passed (103)
```

`oxlint src/cli/capability-cli.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` → clean (exit 0).

**Mutation check (tests are load-bearing):** temporarily reverting the guard back to `Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer())` makes both fail-closed tests fail (`.rejects.toThrow` receives `undefined` because the unbounded path buffers all 64 MiB and succeeds). Restoring the fix → 103/103 pass.

<details>
<summary>Security & Privacy</summary>

- Hardening change: bounds an unbounded in-memory read of an untrusted external response (provider-controlled video URL), closing a memory-exhaustion / DoS vector on the CLI/agent process.
- **No URL leak**: the overflow error reports only the provider label and byte cap — it never interpolates the raw provider URL, which may be signed/tokenized. A dedicated test asserts the error contains neither the URL nor its query token. This matches the sibling generated-media downloaders.
- Fail-closed: overflow cancels the stream and throws; no partial/truncated media is returned and no data is silently dropped.
- No new network calls, no new data persisted, no logging of response bodies or secrets.
- Touches `src/cli/**` only; no `CODEOWNERS` security-owned paths and no shared test mocks were modified.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Compatibility — maintainer acceptance requested</summary>

- Backward compatible for normal use: downloads at or under the cap (default 16 MiB, raisable via `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb`) behave identically; only oversized buffered downloads now error instead of risking OOM.
- **Behavior change requiring maintainer sign-off**: the no-`--output` URL fallback now *fails closed* for videos over the configured cap instead of best-effort buffering them when memory happens to allow it. `--output` remains the streaming route for arbitrarily large videos. Please confirm you accept this fail-closed default for the buffered path.
- No changes to CLI flags, output schema, config schema, or public APIs. No dependency changes; reuses the in-repo `readResponseWithLimit` helper and existing `resolveGeneratedMediaMaxBytes` cap resolver.
- Blast radius: **low** — one source file (`src/cli/capability-cli.ts`, +21/-1) plus self-contained tests using local mocks.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Relation to prior merged work</summary>

This is the CLI buffered-fallback companion to the response-limit campaign: image generation (#96495), OpenRouter video submit (#96873), and the broader #95103 / #95108 / #95218 bounded-read series. Same primitive, same fail-closed convention, same URL-free error style.
</details>

---

AI disclosure: implemented with Claude Code (Claude Opus). Scope of assistance: the model located the existing bounded-read convention and the URL-free sibling error style, wrote the one-call guard replacement and the four focused tests, authored a standalone real-`node:http` proof harness with a negative control, and ran a mutation check; a human author reviews and owns the change and understands what the code does.
target.author: alix-007
